_description: Curated YouTube channels for transcript collection. Rated by quality and relevance to SCBE training domains. Updated daily via free loops.
_policy: Transcripts only (no video download). Fair use for training. Scrub secrets before storage.

channels.name: David Perell
channels.why: Clear thinking about systems, communication, and building in public. High signal-to-noise.

channels.name: 3Blue1Brown
channels.domain: math visualization, linear algebra, topology, calculus
channels.why: Best math explainer on YouTube. Covers hyperbolic geometry, group theory, neural networks — directly relevant to SCBE math.

channels.name: Andrej Karpathy
channels.domain: deep learning, transformers, training, GPT architecture
channels.why: Former Tesla AI lead, OpenAI founding member. Builds things from scratch and explains every step.

channels.name: Yannic Kilcher
channels.domain: ML paper reviews, AI research, industry analysis
channels.why: Deep paper reviews with honest assessment. Good for understanding what research actually claims vs hype.

channels.name: Lex Fridman
channels.why: Long interviews with top researchers. Good for understanding how experts actually think, not just what they publish.

channels.name: Computerphile
channels.why: University-level CS explainers. Strong on crypto, security, and networking fundamentals.

channels.name: LiveOverflow
channels.domain: hacking, CTF, security research, exploit development
channels.why: Best security education channel. Real exploits explained honestly. Perfect for UM tongue training.

channels.name: John Hammond
channels.why: Practical security with real demonstrations. Good complement to LiveOverflow's theory.

channels.name: Fireship
channels.domain: web dev, programming languages, tech news, 100-second explainers
channels.why: Concise, high-density tech explainers. Good for DR (structure) training — how to compress complex ideas.

channels.name: Two Minute Papers
channels.domain: AI research summaries, computer graphics, simulation
channels.why: Accessible AI research summaries. Good for learning how to communicate complex results simply.

channels.name: Veritasium
channels.why: Deep science storytelling. Challenges assumptions. Perfect for teaching models to question and verify.

channels.name: Numberphile
channels.domain: mathematics, number theory, Riemann hypothesis, primes
channels.why: Accessible pure math. Has videos on Riemann hypothesis, phi, Fibonacci — directly relevant.

channels.name: NetworkChuck
channels.why: Practical infrastructure and networking. Good for understanding real-world deployment.

channels.name: Ben Eater
channels.domain: computer engineering, breadboard computers, networking from scratch
channels.why: Builds computers from transistors. The deepest hardware understanding on YouTube. Teaches fundamentals.

channels.name: Sebastian Lague
channels.why: Builds complex systems from scratch with beautiful visualization. Good for teaching structured thinking.

channels.name: Robert Miles
channels.why: The best AI safety explainer. Directly relevant to SCBE governance concepts.

channels.name: CGP Grey
channels.why: Explains complex governance and decision systems clearly. Perfect for KO (intent/control) training.

channels.name: Primer
channels.why: Agent-based simulations showing emergent behavior. Relevant to swarm governance and BFT concepts.
